title: Fire Severity Module. In 'Ecological Field Monitoring Protocols Manual using the Ecological Monitoring System Australia'
author: Laws, Mark
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description:
This module is one of many that form the  Ecological Field Monitoring Protocols Manual    using the  Ecological Monitoring System Australia (EMSA),    available at  https://emsa.tern.org.au/documents . Fire severity is defined as the degree of consumption and scorching of live vegetation and dead fuel, and the degree to which soil properties and processes are altered due to combustion (Smith et al. 2005; Hammill and Bradstock 2006). This module outlines the protocol for recording fire severity within the Core Monitoring Plot (hereafter ‘plot’). The protocol involves recording the cover and height of fire severity attributes from the ground to the canopy at 404 point-intercepts along four 100 m transects within the plot as well as trunk char height at four locations within the plot. 
 The Fire Severity Module involves the collection of quantitative, accurate and repeatable measures of fire severity using the point-intercept method (described in detail in the Cover Module), as well as measurements of trunk char height. Given that the Fire Severity Module and the Cover Module utilise the same point-intercept transects, the two modules are undertaken concurrently. 
 Fire severity is quantified horizontally and vertically within the plot through the following attributes: 
 
 Cover and height of vegetation 
 Cover and height of charred and scorched vegetation 
 Cover and height of resprouting vegetation 
 Cover of litter and coarse woody debris (CWD) 
 Cover of charred litter and CWD 
 Cover of scorched litter (i.e. scorched leaf fall) 
 Cover of bare and charred soil 
 Cover of white and black ash 
 Trunk char height (in wooded systems).
